title: elymus trachycaulus var. major
intro: Elymus trachycaulus var. major, also known as the blue wildrye, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, blue wildrye has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of blue wildrye include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Blue wildrye can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies due to its ease of use and accessibility.
